fact: March 30 is exactly 89 days into the year, which means there are 276 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, cherry blossom trees are typically exploding with pink flowers around this time, creating natural confetti storms when the wind blows!
Squirrels often act extra busy around this time as they frantically search for nuts they buried during winter and dig up old treasure stashes!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around March 30, making it super easy to figure out directions without a compass!
March 30 falls in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac's ram is charging full speed ahead with fiery spring energy!
Earthworms are usually wiggling closer to the surface around this time as the soil warms up after their winter rest!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 30 typically marks serious autumn vibes with leaves turning golden and red!
The daylight on March 30 lasts about 12 hours and 20 minutes in most temperate places, giving you extra time to play outside after school!
Maple trees are pumping sugar-rich sap through their trunks around this time, which is why spring maple syrup tastes so amazing!
March 30 sits exactly 9 days after the spring equinox, when Earth has officially tilted enough to wake up many sleepy plants and animals in northern climates!
Frogs are often croaking their loudest love songs around this date because ponds have warmed up just enough for their spring romance!
The moon phase around March 30 changes every year, but when it's a full moon, it rises right as the sun sets, creating a magical golden hour sandwich!
